message.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Objectives</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>To assess the impact of the implemented social distancing interventions (SD) in Greece.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Study Design</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>A dynamic, discrete time, stochastic individual-based model was developed to simulate COVID-19 transmission.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Methods</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>We fit the transmission model to the observed trends in deaths and ICU beds use.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Results</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>If Greece had not implemented the SD measures, the healthcare system would have been overwhelmed between 30 March and 4 of April. Additionally, the SD interventions averted 4360 deaths and prevent the healthcare system from overwhelmed.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
                <jats:sec>
                  <jats:title>Conclusions</jats:title>
                  <jats:p>The fast reflexes of the Greek government limit the burden of the Covid-19 outbreak.</jats:p>
                </jats:sec>
title: Act early, save lives: managing COVID-19 in Greece
